name: Present Tense
description: Regular and irregular present tense verbs

items.question: Yo ___ (hablar) español todos los días.
items.explanation: With 'yo' (I), regular -ar verbs end in '-o'. Hablar → hablo.

items.question: Tú ___ (comer) mucha fruta.
items.translation: You eat a lot of fruit.
items.explanation: With 'tú' (you, informal), regular -er verbs end in '-es'. Comer → comes.

items.question: Ella ___ (vivir) en Madrid.
items.explanation: With 'ella' (she), regular -ir verbs end in '-e'. Vivir → vive.

items.question: Nosotros ___ (estudiar) español.
items.explanation: With 'nosotros' (we), regular -ar verbs end in '-amos'. Estudiar → estudiamos.

items.question: Ellos ___ (beber) café por la mañana.
items.translation: They drink coffee in the morning.
items.explanation: With 'ellos' (they), regular -er verbs end in '-en'. Beber → beben.

items.question: Yo ___ (tener) dos hermanos.
items.explanation: Tener is irregular. With 'yo', it becomes 'tengo' (g insertion).

items.question: ¿Tú ___ (ir) al parque hoy?
items.translation: Are you going to the park today?
items.explanation: Ir (to go) is highly irregular. Tú → vas.

items.question: Ella ___ (ser) muy inteligente.
items.explanation: Ser (to be, permanent) is irregular. Ella → es.

items.question: Nosotros ___ (hacer) la tarea.
items.explanation: Hacer (to do/make) is irregular in the yo form, but regular for nosotros → hacemos.

items.question: ¿Ustedes ___ (querer) ir al cine?
items.translation: Do you want to go to the cinema?
items.explanation: Querer (to want) has a stem change e→ie. Ustedes → quieren.

items.question: Mi perro ___ (dormir) mucho.
items.explanation: Dormir (to sleep) has stem change o→ue. Third person singular → duerme.

items.question: Yo ___ (poder) hablar tres idiomas.
items.explanation: Poder (to be able to) has stem change o→ue. Yo → puedo.

items.question: Tú ___ (saber) la respuesta.
items.explanation: Saber (to know facts) is irregular in yo form (sé), but regular for tú → sabes.

items.question: Ella ___ (decir) la verdad.
items.explanation: Decir (to say/tell) has stem change e→i and is irregular. Ella → dice.

items.question: Nosotros ___ (estar) contentos.
items.explanation: Estar (to be, temporary/location) is irregular. Nosotros → estamos.

items.question: ___ libro es interesante.
items.explanation: 'Libro' is masculine singular → 'El'. El libro = The book.

items.question: ___ casa es grande.
items.explanation: 'Casa' is feminine singular → 'La'. La casa = The house.

items.question: ___ niños juegan en el parque.
items.translation: The children play in the park.
items.explanation: 'Niños' is masculine plural → 'Los'. Los niños = The children.

items.question: ___ flores son bonitas.
items.explanation: 'Flores' is feminine plural → 'Las'. Las flores = The flowers.

items.question: Quiero ___ café.
items.explanation: 'Café' is masculine → indefinite article 'un'. Un café = A coffee.

items.question: Hay ___ mesa en la cocina.
items.translation: There is a table in the kitchen.
items.explanation: 'Mesa' is feminine → indefinite article 'una'. Una mesa = A table.

items.question: ___ agua está fría.
items.explanation: 'Agua' is feminine but starts with stressed 'a', so uses 'El' to avoid cacophony: El agua.

items.question: Necesito ___ lápices.
items.explanation: 'Lápices' is masculine plural. Indefinite plural → 'unos'. Unos lápices = some pencils.

items.question: ___ problema es difícil.
items.explanation: 'Problema' ends in -a but is masculine (Greek origin). El problema.

items.question: Veo ___ estrellas en el cielo.
items.translation: I see the stars in the sky.
items.explanation: 'Estrellas' is feminine plural → 'las'. Las estrellas = The stars.

items.question: Tengo ___ idea.
items.explanation: 'Idea' is feminine → indefinite article 'una'. Una idea = An idea.

items.question: ___ profesor es amable.
items.explanation: 'Profesor' is masculine singular → 'El'. El profesor = The teacher.

items.question: Hay ___ gatos en el jardín.
items.translation: There are some cats in the garden.
items.explanation: 'Gatos' is masculine plural. Indefinite → 'unos'. Unos gatos = Some cats.

items.question: ___ ciudades españolas son hermosas.
items.explanation: 'Ciudades' is feminine plural → 'Las'. Las ciudades = The cities.

items.question: Quiero ___ manzana.
items.explanation: 'Manzana' is feminine → indefinite article 'una'. Una manzana = An apple.

items.question: Ayer yo ___ (hablar) con mi madre.
items.translation: Yesterday I spoke with my mother.
items.explanation: Preterite of -ar verbs: yo → -é. Hablar → hablé.

items.question: Tú ___ (comer) en un restaurante.
items.explanation: Preterite of -er verbs: tú → -iste. Comer → comiste.

items.question: Ella ___ (escribir) una carta.
items.explanation: Preterite of -ir verbs: ella → -ió. Escribir → escribió.

items.question: Nosotros ___ (viajar) a España.
items.explanation: Preterite nosotros of -ar verbs: → -amos (same as present). Viajar → viajamos.

items.question: Ellos ___ (ir) al cine anoche.
items.translation: They went to the cinema last night.
items.explanation: Ir (to go) is completely irregular in preterite. Ellos → fueron.

items.question: Yo ___ (hacer) la tarea ayer.
items.explanation: Hacer is irregular in preterite with stem 'hic-'. Yo → hice.

items.question: ¿Tú ___ (poder) terminar a tiempo?
items.translation: Were you able to finish on time?
items.explanation: Poder is irregular in preterite with stem 'pud-'. Tú → pudiste.

items.question: Él ___ (tener) un accidente.
items.explanation: Tener is irregular in preterite with stem 'tuv-'. Él → tuvo.

items.question: Nosotros ___ (estar) en la playa.
items.explanation: Estar is irregular in preterite with stem 'estuv-'. Nosotros → estuvimos.

items.question: Ella ___ (decir) la verdad.
items.explanation: Decir is irregular in preterite with stem 'dij-'. Ella → dijo (no accent needed).

items.question: Yo ___ (ser) estudiante.
items.explanation: Ser shares preterite forms with ir. Yo → fui.

items.question: ¿Tú ___ (ver) la película?
items.explanation: Ver in preterite is shortened: tú → viste (no accent, one syllable stem).

items.question: Ellos ___ (saber) la noticia.
items.explanation: Saber is irregular in preterite with stem 'sup-'. Ellos → supieron.

items.question: Ella ___ (poner) los libros en la mesa.
items.translation: She put the books on the table.
items.explanation: Poner is irregular in preterite with stem 'pus-'. Ella → puso.

items.question: Yo ___ (dar) un regalo a mi amigo.
items.translation: I gave a gift to my friend.
items.explanation: Dar is irregular: yo → di (no accent, uses -er/-ir endings).

name: Ser vs Estar
description: Choosing between the two 'to be' verbs

items.question: Ella ___ doctora.
items.explanation: Professions use 'ser'. She IS (permanently/identity) a doctor → es.

items.question: Yo ___ cansado hoy.
items.explanation: Temporary states/feelings use 'estar'. Being tired is temporary → estoy.

items.question: La fiesta ___ en mi casa.
items.translation: The party is at my house.
items.explanation: Event locations use 'ser'. La fiesta es en mi casa.

items.question: El libro ___ en la mesa.
items.translation: The book is on the table.
items.explanation: Physical location of objects uses 'estar'. The book is ON (located at) → está.

items.question: Nosotros ___ de España.
items.explanation: Origin uses 'ser'. We ARE from Spain (permanent origin) → somos.

items.question: La sopa ___ caliente.
items.explanation: Temporary conditions use 'estar'. The soup being hot is a current state → está.

items.question: Madrid ___ la capital de España.
items.translation: Madrid is the capital of Spain.
items.explanation: Defining characteristics use 'ser'. Madrid IS the capital (definition) → es.

items.question: ¿Cómo ___ tú? — Bien, gracias.
items.explanation: Current state/feelings: ¿Cómo estás? (How are you feeling right now) → estás.

items.question: La película ___ interesante.
items.explanation: Inherent characteristics use 'ser'. The movie IS interesting (its nature) → es.

items.question: Ellos ___ contentos con los resultados.
items.translation: They are happy with the results.
items.explanation: Emotions/temporary states use 'estar'. Being happy about results is temporary → están.

items.question: Hoy ___ lunes.
items.explanation: Dates, days, and time use 'ser'. Today IS Monday → es.

items.question: La puerta ___ abierta.
items.explanation: Result of a change/current condition uses 'estar'. The door is (currently) open → está.

items.question: ___ las tres de la tarde.
items.translation: It is three in the afternoon.
items.explanation: Time expressions use 'ser'. For hours > 1 → Son. Son las tres.

items.question: El cielo ___ azul.
items.explanation: Inherent/natural characteristics use 'ser'. The sky IS blue (its nature) → es.

items.question: Mi hermano ___ enfermo.
items.explanation: Illness is a temporary condition → 'estar'. Mi hermano está enfermo.
